methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $6.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Redwood Materials is a Series D climate and energy company founded by J. B. Straubel, currently valued at $6.0B+ on $1.125B+ raised. The broader peer set comprises 1 public company (LNZA) and 8 private frontier-tech peers in climate and energy, including TAE Technologies, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, QuantumScape, and Form Energy.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $6.0B last post-money valuation as a base and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, yielding $9.0B. The peer-median approach anchors to three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: KoBold Metals ($2.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C), producing a median of $1.0B and a range of $700M to $2.0B.

The two methods diverge significantly, reflecting both the company's established scale relative to earlier-stage peers and uncertainty around sustainable valuation multiples in the sector. Triangulation yields a range of $850M (low) to $10.3B (high), with a median of $5.0B. The wide spread underscores the critical unknowns: capital deployment trajectory, unit economics validation, and regulatory/market tailwinds for energy infrastructure. The current $6.0B valuation sits within this range but closer to the lower-median bound, suggesting modest upside under continued execution.
